provenance: RESULT line from executed daemon job a5c3641e log (verbatim below). Two-mode comparison aborted by daemon arg-injection: daemon appended '--lora-path' to the script invocation, argv dispatch routed to a single probe with mode='--lora-path' (= unpinned/native: the 'pinned' branch never fired). That single native run is decisive on both hypotheses.

findings.H1_generation_under_pinned_dsl: CONFIRMED indirectly: native generation = 0 exhausted (48/48) vs t3 production (pinned) = 168 exhausted variants + v1 diag hang pattern (alarm-retry loops, >20min/mode).
findings.H2_solver_verifier_divergence: CONFIRMED directly: re-arc verifiers.py passes 48/48 generated pairs; original arc-dsl solvers pass 25/48 (52%) with 6 errors. verifiers.py is the correct program text for the augmented distribution.
findings.explains_t3_26pct: t3 used solver text on augmented pairs (52% ceiling at diff 0.5, lower at 0.7/1.0) under pinned-dsl generation (exhaustion + corruption).

decision: t3 seed v2 (pending layer): generate under re-arc NATIVE dsl; augmented episodes use verifiers.py program text (same MIT, wrapped like solvers); switch sandbox world-library to re-arc's dsl copy; re-verify ALL episodes (existing 767 included) under the unified dsl. Expected ledger ~2000. Runs parallel; does NOT block tonight's r1 train on the current 767.
lesson_daemon: Eval scripts must not dispatch on argv — the daemon appends its own args (e.g. --lora-path). Use env vars or self-contained mains.
